title: podman machine start: make checkExclusiveActiveVM check race free

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Though your test appears to be failing on WSL

```
  C:\Users\Administrator\AppData\Local\cirrus-ci-build\repo\bin\windows\podman.exe machine start b961317beebe
  Starting machine "b961317beebe"
  time="2024-03-13T17:06:50Z" level=warning msg="detected port conflict on machine ssh port [50092], reassigning"
  your 131072x1 screen size is bogus. expect trouble

  This machine is currently configured in rootless mode. If your containers
  require root permissions (e.g. ports < 1024), or if you run into compatibility
  issues with non-podman clients, you can switch using the following command:

  	podman machine set --rootful b961317beebe

  API forwarding for Docker API clients is not available due to the following startup failures.
  	could not start api proxy since expected pipe is not available: podman-b961317beebe

  Podman clients are still able to connect.
  Error: machine did not transition into running state: ssh error: machine is not listening on ssh port
  [FAILED] Expected
      <string>: time="2024-03-13T17:06:50Z" level=warning msg="detected port conflict on machine ssh port [50092], reassigning"
      your 131072x1 screen size is bogus. expect trouble
      API forwarding for Docker API clients is not available due to the following startup failures.
      	could not start api proxy since expected pipe is not available: podman-b961317beebe
      
      Podman clients are still able to connect.
      Error: machine did not transition into running state: ssh error: machine is not listening on ssh port
      
  to contain substring
      <string>: VM already running or starting
  In [It] at: C:/Users/Administrator/AppData/Local/cirrus-ci-build/repo/pkg/machine/e2e/start_test.go:88 @ 03/13/24 17:07:12.598

  Full Stack Trace
    github.com/containers/podman/v5/pkg/machine/e2e_test.init.func15.5()
    	C:/Users/Administrator/AppData/Local/cirrus-ci-build/repo/pkg/machine/e2e/start_test.go:88 +0x52e
  < Exit [It] start machine already started - C:/Users/Administrator/AppData/Local/cirrus-ci-build/repo/pkg/machine/e2e/start_test.go:70 @ 03/13/24 17:07:12.598 (34.4s)
```

No idea what this is about but I cannot see how this is related to my changes.

Uh mac failed as well but this looks like a legit test bug there: https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4904265781608448/html/machine-mac-podman-darwin-rootless-host-sqlite.log.html#t--podman-machine-start-start-two-machines-in-parallel--1

I think I know what is wrong, maybe that is the same issue on windows.
Failure is consistent. Is WSL VM startup conflict detection broken?
@Luap99 hit me up in the morning if you don't have this pinned down.  I should be able to help.

I think I see where I went wrong here, as I moved all the checks into the checkExclusiveActiveVM() but skip that for wsl it means it will do nothing to prevent wsl from starting the same machine twice thus the very confusing test behaviour.
Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.
Ok looks like everything should be working now.

@mheon: new pull request created: #22047

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/22032#issuecomment-1997956156):

>/cherry-pick v5.0


Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
